reasoning: Tan Zhongyi's Elo rating on 2026-01-08 was 2524 according to the January 2026 FIDE rating list. A 1% increase would require her to have an Elo rating of at least 2549.24 (effectively 2550). FIDE officially updates its ratings on the 1st of every month. For the first resolution date, 2026-01-25, the January rating list will still be in effect, so her rating shown on Wikipedia will almost certainly remain strictly at 2524, making the outcome effectively impossible (assigning the minimum possible probability of 0.02). For the second date, 2026-02-17, the February rating list will be reflected. Gaining 26 Elo points in a single month is extremely rare for a top-10 female player—it requires playing a large number of classical games and scoring heavily above expectations (e.g., a TPR of nearly 2700 over 9-11 games). There is no evidence she participated in a major classical tournament in January where this would be possible (Tata Steel India was rapid/blitz). I assign highly confident low probabilities for both dates, with a slight adjustment towards 0.5 for the later date due to longer time horizon uncertainty.
